summary: Iter-111 ForgeSlicer beginner CAD batch tested end-to-end via live Playwright on /workspace. iPad Delete in Inspector, Tolerance helper gating + math, Hole/Countersink dialog with M3-M8 + #4-#10 presets all PASS. RANSAC Phase-5 sensitivity slider verified by code review (live trigger requires uploading an imported STL — not done per request guidance). Regression on iter-108/109/110 (Lib tab, Distribute, OrcaDialog) is clean.

phases_results.phase1_ipad_delete_simple: PASS — Inspector renders Drop / Lay Flat / Delete in a 3-col grid. data-testid='inspector-delete-btn' is visible after selecting a cube. Click removes the object silently (no confirm), objects.length 1 → 0.
phases_results.phase2_ipad_delete_group_prompts: PASS — After dropping M3 Standoff (2 grouped parts), selecting one and clicking inspector-delete-btn fires window.confirm with the exact message: 'Delete "Standoff body" from "M3 Standoff"? The rest of the group stays.' Accept removes only that part; Standoff bore remains. Final objects.length = 1.
phases_results.phase3_tolerance_gating: PASS — tolerance-helper is NOT rendered for positive cube (count=0) nor positive cylinder (count=0). It IS rendered for negative cylinder (count=1) and ALSO for negative cone (count=1, consistent with the spec).
phases_results.phase4_tolerance_nudge: PASS — Negative cylinder dims.r=3 → click tolerance-slip (+0.1 mm diametral / 2) → r=3.05 exact. Then tolerance-press (-0.05 mm / 2) → r=3.025 exact. Math matches the half-diameter rule.
phases_results.phase5_hole_dialog_opens: PASS — Composites tab shows add-hole-dialog-btn. Click opens hole-dialog with header 'Hole / Countersink'. All 9 presets present (m3, m4, m5, m6, m8, #4, #6, #8, #10). m3 default selected with cyan border ring (className contains 'border-cyan-400').
phases_results.phase6_preset_switches_dims: PASS — Click hole-preset-m5 → Bore Ø input reads '5.50' (M5 clearance, boreR=2.75 × 2). Click hole-preset-#6 → Bore Ø reads '3.70' (#6 clearance, boreR=1.85 × 2). Input uses toFixed(2) formatting, NumField step=0.05.
phases_results.phase7_countersink_drop: PASS — With M3 selected and countersink toggle ON, hole-add-btn drops exactly 2 grouped parts: 'CS Bore' + 'CS Cup', both sharing groupName='M3 Countersink' and the same groupId. Dialog closes immediately after.
phases_results.phase8_plain_hole_drop: PASS — With M4 + countersink toggle OFF, the Customise disclosure correctly hides hole-input-head-d and hole-input-sink-h. hole-add-btn drops exactly 1 object: name='M4 Hole', type='cylinder', modifier='negative', groupId=null, dims.r=2.20.
phases_results.phase9_ransac_sensitivity_slider: CODE-REVIEW PASS — In ReverseEngineerDialog.jsx: data-testid='re-sensitivity' wrapper renders only after `result` is set (post-segmentation). data-testid='re-sensitivity-slider' is type=range min=0.05 max=2.0 step=0.05 default=0.2 bound to pendingEpsFracPct. data-testid='re-rescan-btn' is conditionally rendered when Math.abs(pendingEpsFracPct - epsFracPct) > 0.001. Click invokes runScan(pendingEpsFracPct) which awaits segmentImportedObject({ epsFrac: epsPct/100 }) and on success sets epsFracPct = epsPct → button disappears (pending == committed). busy state shows a Loader2 spinner inside the button while in-flight. Logic is correct. Live run not exercised because Phase 9 requires an STL upload that goes through the imported-mesh path — request stated code-review-only is acceptable.
phases_results.phase10_regression: PASS — All 5 LeftPanel tabs (3d, 2d, composites, library, ai) clickable and switch palette body. Distribute row still present in the Align popover for 3 selected cubes (distribute-x visible). Component Library still shows 8 cards + 7 category chips (M3 Standoff drop verified during Phase 2). OrcaDialog gate logic unchanged. No console errors during the run.

critical_code_review_comments: HoleDialog.jsx: HOLE_PRESETS array only ships 5 metric (M3/M4/M5/M6/M8) — original problem statement lists M3-M8 which would also include M7, but the header copy says 'Metric (ISO 7045)' and ISO 7045 does not standardise M7, so the omission is intentional. Same for imperial — #4/#6/#8/#10 with no odd sizes. Worth noting in docs.

HoleDialog.jsx onAddHole 'plain' branch: a new negative cylinder is built via `buildPrimitive('cylinder','negative')` and then overrides position+dims+name, but it does NOT push to history (the early `pushHistory()` is called before the setState mutation, so undo works). The composite branch delegates to `addCountersink` which has its own pushHistory. Both are undo-atomic — verified by the iter-110 component-undo regression. OK.
RightPanel.jsx ToleranceHelper: applyFit clamps with Math.max(0.05, …) so radius can never go to 0 or negative — sensible safety net for repeated 'press' clicks on a tiny bore.
RightPanel.jsx inspector-delete-btn: confirm only fires when obj.groupId is truthy — matches spec. Silent delete for single parts is the right UX trade-off.

ReverseEngineerDialog.jsx: useEffect resets pendingEpsFracPct to DEFAULT each time the dialog re-opens — good. runScan is wrapped in useCallback with [obj] dep, so the auto-run effect doesn't re-fire on every state update.
Pre-existing lint warnings in RightPanel.jsx (escape-entity) are unchanged from iter-110 and not introduced by this iteration.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (9 live phases + 1 code-review phase, all pass)

test_credentials: session_token=st_main_1782619353564 on orca-cad-slice.preview.emergentagent.com — still valid for this run.
seed_data_creation: All test scene objects created and torn down via window.__forgeStore.setState; no persisted seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter-111 adds: (1) inspector-delete-btn in RightPanel's 3-button row (Drop/Lay Flat/Delete); (2) ToleranceHelper component shown only when obj.modifier=='negative' && obj.type in ('cylinder','cone'), with 5 fits (press/tight/slip/running/loose); (3) HoleDialog (new file in dialogs/) launched from LeftPanel Combo tab via add-hole-dialog-btn; (4) RANSAC sensitivity slider in ReverseEngineerDialog. window.__forgeStore.setState (NOT getState().setState) is the right way to mutate scene from tests. Dialog confirm() prompts on inspector-delete-btn ONLY for grouped parts — use page.on('dialog', d=>d.accept()) before clicking. Default leftpanel.tab persists in localStorage 'forge.leftpanel.tab' — set back to '3d' between runs if your tests assume default tab.
